{{Quote|Aaaargh!|Halmere|<ref name="ear" />}}
'''Halmere''' was a [[High Inquisitor]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. At the conclusion of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], Halmere was one of the many [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] adepts held by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] on the [[Deep Core/Legends|Deep Core]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Byss/Legends|Byss]]. There, he and his fellow captives were tested by the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]], [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], to determine whether or not they could be of use to the Empire. Halmere, along with [[Antinnis Tremayne]], [[Lanu Pasiq]], and [[Gwellib Ap-Llewff]], were selected by Vader, while the rest were [[Death/Legends|killed]]. Subsequently, the four initiates were made [[Inquisitorius/Legends|Inquisitors]], servants of Vader and his newly-formed Inquisitorius.

Trained by Vader himself, the Inquisitors were soon tasked with hunting down a rogue [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] who was hiding on [[Coruscant/Legends|Imperial Center]]. Identified as [[Drake Lo'gaan/Legends|Jodd Sonta]], Halmere, Tremayne, and the others confronted the fugitive and his Jedi allies, [[Ekria/Legends|Ekria]] and [[Zonder/Legends|Zonder]], but were foiled during the ensuing skirmish, allowing the criminals to escape. Many [[Time/Legends|years]] later, shortly after the [[Dissolution of the Imperial Senate/Legends|Dissolution of the Imperial Senate]], Halmere was tasked by [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] with interviewing former [[Senator/Legends|Senator]] [[Canna Omonda]] of [[Chandrila/Legends|Chandrila]], who was a vocal critic of the [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]]. During the ensuing interview with Halmere, Omonda admitted to [[Treason/Legends|high treason]], and was later sentenced to death.

==Biography==
===Recruitment===
{{Quote|Halmere, get the names of everyone in this room.|Antinnis Tremayne|<ref name="ear" />}}
A [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]], Halmere was amongst a large group of [[Agricultural Corps/Legends|Agricultural Corps]] members and other [[The Force/Legends|Force]] adepts who had survived the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], [[Prisoner/Legends|imprisoned]] by the newly formed [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and overseen by [[Sly Moore/Legends|Sly Moore]], a member of the Imperial government. They were held on the [[Deep Core/Legends|Deep Core]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Byss/Legends|Byss]] at the insistence of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]], who hoped to have some of them made to serve the Empire. Not long after the [[Declaration of a New Order]] that had given rise to the Empire, both Palpatine and his [[Sith apprentice/Legends|apprentice]], the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], inspected the captives. Palpatine, before departing, released the captives' binding, setting them on Vader as a test. Vader, who had [[Death/Legends|killed]] many [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] in the prior [[Standard month/Legends|months]], goaded the captives, and they rushed him with makeshift weapons. Halmere was amongst the attackers, but Vader was easily able to fend off the adepts, until one captive, [[Antinnis Tremayne]], turned off the Dark Lord's [[Darth Vader's armor/Legends|breathing device]]. Satisfied, Vader selected Tremayne and Halmere, as well as [[Lanu Pasiq]] and [[Gwellib Ap-Llewff]] to serve him, before having the rest of the captives executed immediately.<ref name="ear" />

[[File:Inquisitortraining.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Halmere and the other Inquisitors training under Darth Vader.]]
The four selected adepts became Inquisitors in the newly formed [[Inquisitorius/Legends|Inquisitorius]]. Halmere accompanied Tremayne, Pasiq, and Ap-Llewff to the [[Jrade District/Legends|Jrade District]] on [[Coruscant/Legends|Imperial Center]], where Tremayne addressed a meeting of the [[Commission for the Preservation of the New Order/Legends|Commission for the Preservation of the New Order's]] [[Sub-Adult Group/Legends|Sub-Adult Group]]. When Tremayne finished his speech, he turned to Halmere and had him acquire the names of all those present, as he had detected a Force-sensitive in the crowd. Later, Halmere participated in a sparring session with his fellow Inquisitors, overseen by Vader. During the session, Tremayne told Vader that he had detected the presence of a [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] at the Sub-Adult meeting, which correlated with reports from the planet's [[Coruscant Underworld/Legends|lower levels]]. Halmere, Pasiq and Ap-Llewff were all outstripped by Tremayne during the session, who managed to [[Telekinesis/Legends|push]] Pasiq directly into Halmere. By the [[Time/Legends|time]] Tremayne had disarmed his fellow Inquisitors and finished his report, Vader simply ordered him to rid [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] of the Jedi.<ref name="ear" />

===Hunting===
{{Quote|Selonian, you are wanted on Imperial business.|Halmere to Zonder|<ref name="ear" />}}
[[File:ZonderBitesHalmere-Recruitment.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Zonder bites Halmere.]]
Tremayne was later able to divine that the Padawan was one [[Drake Lo'gaan/Legends|Jodd Sonta]], and that he had a [[Selonian/Legends|Selonian]] Jedi companion, [[Zonder/Legends|Zonder]]. The Selonian, however, was to unwittingly depart for Byss to work as a [[Slavery/Legends|slave]], and Tremayne was confident that Sonta would try and mount a rescue attempt. Tremayne boarded the slave [[Transport/Legends|transport]] alone to trap Sonta, while Halmere, Pasiq, and Ap-Llewff waited for orders. They eventually were given the command to kill Zonder, and they moved in, [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]] drawn. The Inquisitors found the Selonian accompanied by another Jedi fugitive, [[Ekria/Legends|Ekria]], and a band of other non-Human slaves. Pasiq demanded that the group surrender, but they were defiant, and attacked the Inquisitors. Zonder, leaping forward, bit Halmere's [[Limb/Legends|arm]], forcing the Inquisitor to drop his blaster. Pasiq and Ap-Llewff were also subdued, as Halmere was attacked by an [[Ithorian/Legends|Ithorian]].<ref name="ear" />

[[Stormtrooper/Legends|Stormtroopers]] then entered the area, and opened fire on the Jedi and the slaves. Halmere, Pasiq, and Ap-Llewff, however, were incapacitated, and unable to give chase as their opponents retreated back into the transport. The criminals were then able to escape on a [[Bantha-II cargo skiff/Legends|Bantha-II cargo skiff]], and Sonta was able to evade Tremayne's grasp. As the fugitives escaped and the Inquisitors were left empty-handed, Tremayne reminded his colleagues that as Vader was off-planet, their failure was to be kept a secret.<ref name="ear" />

===Interrogation of Canna Omonda===
{{Quote|According to Spinbalio, Halmere and his aides were quite surprised when Omonda began sobbing shortly into the interview.|Coruscant Daily Newsfeed|<ref name="nn">{{JournalCite|4|Galaxywide NewsNets}}</ref>}}
By [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]], Halmere had risen to the rank of [[High Inquisitor]]. Shortly after the [[Dissolution of the Imperial Senate/Legends|Dissolution of the Imperial Senate]], former [[Senator/Legends|Imperial Senator]] [[Canna Omonda]] of [[Chandrila/Legends|Chandrila]] spoke out against [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] Palpatine in the media. According to a [[Coruscant Daily Newsfeed/Legends|Coruscant Daily Newsfeed]] report, Palpatine requested that Omonda return from Chandrila to Imperial Center for an interview with Inquisitor Halmere. Omonda complied, and was given an escort of three [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|Imperial Star Destroyers]] for her journey. During the interview, Omonda reportedly began sobbing not long after the commencement of the session, and proceeded to claim that she, as an Imperial Senator of a [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core World]], had used her access to classified information, and passed it on to agents of the dissident [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]], which at that time was [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|at war]] with the Empire. Omonda proceeded to divulge the names and locations of her contacts, who were arrested shortly thereafter. A spokeswoman from Halmere's office, [[Gretta Spinbalio]], passed on the details of the interview to the media. Subsequently, Omonda was sentenced to be executed during [[Fete Week]] for high treason.<ref name="nn" />

===Later Career===
[[File:GreatInquestor.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Halmere as the Zenithal InQuestor of Judgment]]
Halmere soon advanced through the ranks of the Inquisitors and become one of the [[High Inquisitor]]s. After the vacuum caused by the death of Palpatine on [[Endor/Legends|Endor]], he, like many of his compeers, was recruited by High Inquisitor [[Jerec (Inquisitor)|Jerec]] in to the [[Pentastar Alignment]], the splinter state set up by [[Grand Moff/Legends|Grand Moff]] [[Ardus Kaine]]. There he became a [[Great InQuestor of Judgment]], that regime's version of an Inquisitor, and inherited the title of [[Zenithal InQuestor of Judgment]] after his predecessor Jerec was struck down.<ref name="iwda" />

==Personality and traits==
Halmere was prone to screaming out in pain when bitten on the arm by large Selonians. He also registered surprise when disarmed by his sparring partners.<ref name="ear" /> According to Coruscant Daily Newsfeed, Halmere was startled when Senator Omonda confessed her crimes to him during an interview.<ref name="nn" />

==Powers and abilities==
Halmere displayed enough aptitude with the Force to be selected from the group of captives on Byss by Darth Vader, for service in the Inquisitorius. He was, however, outmatched in [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|lightsaber combat]] by his fellow Inquisitor, Tremayne. Even when armed with a blaster, Halmere was unable to defeat an unarmed Jedi Padawan, demonstrated when he was confronted by the Selonian Zonder.<ref name="ear" />

==Behind the scenes==
Inquisitor Halmere was first mentioned in the "[[Galaxywide NewsNets]]" section of [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 4|''Star Wars Adventure Journal'' 4]], which was published in [[1994]]. The character went unused in the [[Star Wars Legends|Expanded Universe]] for 11 years, until he made his first appearance in [[Paul Ens]]' ''[[Evasive Action: Recruitment]]''. For the [[Star Wars comic books|comic]], [[Tom Hodges|Thomas Hodges]] provided the first rendering of Halmere. The character was later given an entry in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', published in [[2008]]. In [[2013]], Halmere was referenced in the [[Star Wars Blog]] article ''[[The Imperial Warlords: Despoilers of an Empire]]'', which mentioned that he had joined the Pentastar Alignment and become a Grand InQuestor of Judgment. The picture in the article identified as Halmere was originally published in ''[[The Pentastar Alignment]]'', an article in [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 3|''Star Wars Adventure Journal'' 3]]. The picture was a representation of the Grand InQuestors from the Pentastar Alignment, and the InQuestor was unidentified in the original article. The picture was republished in ''[[The Best of the Star Wars Adventure Journal, Issues 1-4]]'' and colored, as a representation of [[Chris Gossett]]'s artwork.
